content.full_text:
LEON E. PANETTA
18TH DISTRICT, CALIFORNIA

WASHINGTON OFFICE
339 CANNON HOUSE OFFICE BUILDING
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20515
(202) 225-2861

CHAIRMAN
HOUSE BUDGET COMMITTEE

Congress of the United States
House of Representatives
Washington, DC 20515-0516

COMMITTEES
AGRICULTURE
HOUSE ADMINISTRATION
SELECT COMMITTEE ON HUNGER
STEERING AND POLICY

MAJORITY WHIP
FOR THE BUDGET

DISTRICT OFFICES
MONTEREY STREET
MONTEREY, CA 93940
(408) 648-3555

HOLLISTER, CA
(408) 637-0600

SALINAS, CA
(408) 424-2229

SAN LUIS OBISPO, CA
(805) 541-0143

SANTA CRUZ, CA
(408) 429-1876

December 2, 1992

TO:     Office of the Chairman
        Fair Political Practices Commission
        Post Office Box 807
        Sacramento, California 95804


ENCLOSURE FROM:    Richard Duchscher.

RE:     Mr. Duchscher would like to know if it is possible for
        a political campaign (for the Board of a local agency)
        to have official non-profit status.

        Would you please review the attached and reply to the concerns
        or questions which this constituent has brought to my attention?

        Thank you for your assistance. Please reply in writing and/or
        with printed information.




Thank you very much for your attention to this matter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        LEON E. PANETTA
                                        Member of Congress

PLEASE RESPOND TO ME AT:

380 Alvarado Street
Monterey, California 93940
        Ken Christopher; (408) 429-1976
ATTENTION:

                        PRINTED ON RECYCLED PAPER

CONSTITUENT REQUEST

DATE:                    11/16/92

STAFF MEMBER:           kwc                                          B

CONSTITUENT NAME:       Mr. Richard Duchscher

ADDRESS:               152 Danube Dr.
                       Aptos, CA  95003


PHONE:                 688-9700

Position/A_____ Information/B___ B  Bill Status/C_____ Document/D____

                    VIEWPOINT OR REQUEST

Issue/Subject          Non-profit status.

1.  Mr. Duchscher would like to know how to get non-profit status for
    a political campaign, and whether it is possible to do so.
    This would be a campaign for a seat on a local Fire District
    Board.

2.  Mr. Duchscher understands that the Postal Service offers low-cost
    mail (11¢/letter v. 29¢) to non-profit organizations.
    He would like to know how to apply to the Postal Service
    for this special non-profit permit.
